message:
feat(fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-cbor): glob-style resource constraints, zero-copy frame decode, CBOR buffer prealloc

Capability resource matching now uses real glob patterns instead of
prefix-only starts_with checks, enabling patterns like "/api/v1/*" and
"/admin/*" with proper wildcard semantics including single-char "?"
wildcards. The pattern_matches engine was rewritten as a byte-level
state machine supporting *, ?, and literal matching with backtracking.

FCPS frame decoding gains a zero-copy path via SymbolRecordRef and
FcpsFrameRefs that borrow symbol data directly from the frame buffer,
eliminating one Vec<u8> heap allocation per symbol record on
decode-and-forward paths. Benchmarks added for the new decode_refs path.

SignedFcpsFrame::encode() and verify() no longer panic on invalid state;
they return Result with proper error types. Source ID length is validated
at construction and verification time via a dedicated FrameError variant.

Canonical CBOR serialization pre-allocates a 256-byte buffer to avoid
reallocation for typical small payloads.

- policy.rs: byte-level glob matcher with * and ? support
- capability.rs: use pattern_matches for resource_allow/resource_deny
- capability_golden_vectors.rs: updated patterns to glob syntax
- fcps.rs: SymbolRecordRef, FcpsFrameRefs, panic-free encode/verify
- fcps_parsing.rs: benchmark for zero-copy frame decode
- fcp-cbor/lib.rs: Vec::with_capacity(256) for canonical CBOR output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
